question: Will NASA award a contract to a new commercial partner to develop a spacesuit by mid 2026?
reason: NASA's recent actions indicate a strong likelihood of awarding a new spacesuit contract by mid-2026.
evidence: In June 2024, NASA and Collins Aerospace mutually agreed to end their task orders for spacesuit development due to schedule delays and performance issues. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/spacestation/2024/06/26/nasa-collins-xevas-update/?utm_source=openai))

Following Collins' exit, NASA has been actively seeking new commercial partners to develop next-generation spacesuits for both International Space Station (ISS) operations and Artemis lunar missions. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/news-release/nasa-expands-options-for-spacewalking-moonwalking-suits-services/?utm_source=openai))

As of April 2026, NASA has been challenged to ensure readiness for the Artemis lunar surface mission in 2028 and the ISS before its planned decommission in 2030, indicating a pressing need for new spacesuit contracts. ([oig.nasa.gov](https://oig.nasa.gov/audits/nasas-acquisition-of-next-generation-spacesuit-services/?utm_source=openai))
